title: TOPIK I Level 1 Full Sample Exam

sections.title: Listening
sections.instructions: Read the transcript as the listening stimulus and choose the best answer.

questions.prompt: What is the speaker saying?

explanation.summary: 안녕하세요 is the standard polite greeting for hello.
teaching_points: Use 안녕하세요 when meeting someone in a polite everyday situation.
It can be used in the morning, afternoon, or evening.

grammar.explanation: A polite ending often heard in greetings and polite expressions.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 안녕하세요 with 안녕히 가세요, which is said when someone leaves.

questions.prompt: What does the woman buy?

explanation.summary: 빵 means bread, so the woman asks for one bread item.
teaching_points: 하나 is the native Korean number one.
주세요 is useful when asking for an item in a store.

grammar.explanation: A polite request meaning please give me N.

common_mistakes: Do not choose milk or coffee just because the sentence is in a shop context; the named item is 빵.

questions.prompt: Where is the speaker?

explanation.summary: 도서관에서 means at the library, and 책을 읽어요 means I read a book.
teaching_points: -에서 marks the place where an action happens.
The action 읽어요 helps confirm the library setting.

grammar.explanation: Marks the location of an action.

common_mistakes: Do not use only the verb 읽어요 to guess; the location word 도서관 gives the answer.

questions.prompt: What time is the class?
questions.passage: Transcript: 한국어 수업은 아홉 시에 시작해요.

explanation.summary: 아홉 시 means nine o'clock, so the Korean class starts at 9.
teaching_points: Native Korean numbers are used with 시 for the hour.
시작해요 means starts.

grammar.explanation: Marks the topic of the sentence.

common_mistakes: Do not mix up 아홉 with 여덟; 아홉 is nine and 여덟 is eight.

questions.prompt: How is the weather today?

explanation.summary: 비가 와요 literally means rain comes, the Korean expression for it is raining.
teaching_points: Weather expressions often use 오다, meaning to come.
오늘은 sets today as the topic.

grammar.explanation: Idiomatic weather expression meaning it rains.

common_mistakes: Do not translate 와요 as only physical movement; with rain it means raining.

questions.prompt: What does the man want to drink?
questions.passage: Transcript: 남자: 저는 물을 마시고 싶어요.

explanation.summary: 물을 마시고 싶어요 means I want to drink water.
teaching_points: 물 is water.
-고 싶어요 expresses wanting to do an action.

grammar.explanation: Expresses the speaker's desire to do an action.

common_mistakes: Do not choose coffee just because 마시다 can be used for many drinks; 물 is the object.

questions.prompt: What is Mina's job?

explanation.summary: 학생이에요 means is a student.
teaching_points: 씨 is a polite title used after a name.
-이에요 attaches after a final consonant.
common_mistakes: Do not read 학생 as teacher; teacher is 선생님.

questions.prompt: What day is it tomorrow?

explanation.summary: 토요일 means Saturday, and 내일 means tomorrow.
teaching_points: Days of the week end in 요일.
내일 tells you the sentence is about tomorrow.

grammar.explanation: Polite statement meaning it is N.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 토요일 with 일요일; 일요일 is Sunday.

questions.prompt: What will the speaker do after school?

explanation.summary: 친구를 만나요 means meet a friend.
teaching_points: 후에 means after.
The object marker -를 marks 친구 as the person being met.
common_mistakes: Do not focus only on 학교; the action after school is 만나요.

questions.prompt: How much is the notebook?

explanation.summary: 천 원 means one thousand won.
teaching_points: Sino-Korean numbers are used for prices.
공책 means notebook.

grammar.explanation: A simple way to state the price of an item.

questions.prompt: Choose the best response.

explanation.summary: 고맙습니다 means thank you, and 아니에요 can politely mean you are welcome.
teaching_points: 아니에요 literally means it is not, but it is commonly used after thanks.
The other options do not respond naturally to thanks.

grammar.explanation: Means is not N; as a phrase, 아니에요 can politely dismiss thanks.

common_mistakes: Do not answer 고맙습니다 with 처음 뵙겠습니다; that phrase is for a first meeting.

questions.prompt: Why can't the woman go?
questions.passage: Transcript: 여자: 오늘은 바빠요. 그래서 못 가요.

options.text: She does not know the place.

explanation.summary: 바빠요 means busy, and 그래서 explains the result: she cannot go.
teaching_points: 못 before a verb means cannot do that action.
그래서 connects a reason to a result.
common_mistakes: Do not infer sickness; the stated reason is 바빠요.

questions.prompt: What does the speaker like?

explanation.summary: 사과를 좋아해요 means I like apples.
teaching_points: 좋아해요 is used for liking something.
사과 means apple.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse 사과 with apology; in this sentence it is the fruit apple.

questions.prompt: Where does the speaker live?

explanation.summary: 서울에 살아요 means I live in Seoul.
teaching_points: 살아요 means live.
-에 is used with 살다 for where someone lives.
common_mistakes: With 살다, the place marker is usually -에, not -에서.

questions.prompt: What is in the bag?

explanation.summary: 지갑이 있어요 means there is a wallet.
teaching_points: 안에 means inside.
있어요 indicates existence.

grammar.explanation: Means there is N or I have N.

common_mistakes: Do not answer bag; the bag is the location, not the object inside.

questions.prompt: What does the speaker do on weekends?

explanation.summary: 영화를 봐요 means watch a movie.
teaching_points: 보다 means to see or watch.
주말에 means on the weekend.

grammar.explanation: Can mark time, as in on the weekend.

common_mistakes: 영화 is movie; 운동 is exercise or sports.

questions.prompt: Who is coming to the house?
questions.passage: Transcript: 오늘 저녁에 어머니가 집에 와요.

explanation.summary: 어머니가 집에 와요 means mother comes home.
teaching_points: 어머니 is polite or formal for mother.
집에 와요 means comes to the house/home.

grammar.explanation: Uses -에 to mark the destination of coming.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 어머니 with 아버지, which means father.

questions.prompt: What does the speaker ask for?

explanation.summary: 연필 좀 빌려 주세요 means please lend me a pencil.
teaching_points: 빌려 주세요 is a polite request to borrow something.
좀 softens the request.

grammar.explanation: Polite request asking someone to do an action.

common_mistakes: Do not choose book because this is a school context; the object is 연필.

questions.prompt: What does the woman order?

explanation.summary: 김밥하고 물 means gimbap and water.
teaching_points: 하고 can connect nouns like and.
주세요 makes the order polite.

grammar.explanation: Connects nouns, meaning N and N.

common_mistakes: Do not omit 물; the woman orders two things.

questions.prompt: What is the speaker's phone number ending?

explanation.summary: The number is 1234-5678, so the ending is 5678.
teaching_points: 전화번호 means phone number.
Listen for the second group when asked for the ending.
common_mistakes: Do not answer the first group, 1234, when the question asks for the ending.

questions.prompt: What will the two people do?
questions.passage: Transcript: 남자: 같이 점심 먹을까요? 여자: 좋아요.

explanation.summary: 같이 점심 먹을까요 asks shall we eat lunch together, and 좋아요 accepts.
teaching_points: 같이 means together.
-을까요 can suggest doing something together.

grammar.explanation: Can ask for an opinion or make a suggestion.

common_mistakes: 좋아요 here means agreement, not a separate comment about taste or quality.

questions.prompt: Where will the woman go first?
questions.passage: Transcript: 여자: 먼저 은행에 가요. 그 다음에 시장에 가요.

explanation.summary: 먼저 은행에 가요 means she goes to the bank first.
teaching_points: 먼저 means first.
그 다음에 means after that.
common_mistakes: Do not choose 시장; it is the second destination.

questions.prompt: What is the woman's problem?
questions.passage: Transcript: 여자: 감기에 걸렸어요. 목이 아파요.

options.text: She has a cold and her throat hurts.

explanation.summary: 감기에 걸렸어요 means she caught a cold, and 목이 아파요 means her throat hurts.
teaching_points: 아파요 means hurts or is sick.
Body parts use -이/가 before 아파요.
common_mistakes: 감기 is illness, not weather; the sentence is about health.

questions.prompt: What does the man need?
questions.passage: Transcript: 남자: 오늘 비가 와요. 우산이 필요해요.

explanation.summary: 우산이 필요해요 means I need an umbrella.
teaching_points: 필요해요 means need.
The rain sentence gives context, but 우산 is the needed object.
common_mistakes: Do not answer rain; rain is the reason, not the needed item.

questions.prompt: What is the woman looking for?
questions.passage: Transcript: 여자: 제 휴대전화가 없어요. 어디 있어요?

explanation.summary: 휴대전화가 없어요 means the mobile phone is missing or not here.
teaching_points: 없어요 means there is not or I do not have.
어디 있어요 asks where something is.

grammar.explanation: Means there is no N or I do not have N.

common_mistakes: Do not choose notebook because 공책 is notebook; 휴대전화 is phone.

questions.prompt: What did the speaker do yesterday?

explanation.summary: 어제 means yesterday, and 쉬었어요 means rested.
teaching_points: -었어요 marks past tense here.
집에서 shows the action happened at home.
common_mistakes: Do not treat 쉬었어요 as present tense; the ending shows past action.

questions.prompt: What season does the speaker like?

explanation.summary: 봄을 제일 좋아해요 means I like spring the most.
teaching_points: 제일 means the most.
봄 is spring.

grammar.explanation: Means I like N the most.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 봄 with 여름; 여름 means summer.

questions.prompt: What color bag does the speaker have?

explanation.summary: 파란 가방 means a blue bag.
teaching_points: Color adjectives often change form before a noun.
있어요 can mean have.

grammar.explanation: Descriptive words can come before nouns.

common_mistakes: 파란 is blue; 빨간 is red.

questions.prompt: What does the man ask about?

explanation.summary: 화장실이 어디예요 asks where the restroom is.
teaching_points: 어디예요 means where is it.
This is a useful pattern for asking locations.
common_mistakes: Do not answer with a place name; the question asks what he asks about.

questions.prompt: What is the announcement about?
questions.passage: Transcript: 안내 말씀 드립니다. 버스가 십 분 후에 옵니다.

options.text: The bus will come in ten minutes.

explanation.summary: 버스가 십 분 후에 옵니다 means the bus comes after ten minutes.
teaching_points: 십 분 means ten minutes.
후에 means after; together, 십 분 후에 means in ten minutes.

grammar.explanation: Means after a length of time or in that amount of time.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 십 분 with 십 시; 분 is minutes and 시 is o'clock.

sections.title: Reading
sections.instructions: Read each sentence, notice, message, or short passage and choose the best answer.

questions.prompt: Choose the meaning of the sentence.

explanation.summary: 안 before 마셔요 makes the verb negative, so the sentence means I do not drink coffee.
teaching_points: 안 is a common short negation before verbs.
마셔요 means drink.

grammar.explanation: Short negative form meaning do not V.

common_mistakes: Do not ignore 안; without it the sentence would mean I drink coffee.

questions.prompt: Where does the person go?

explanation.summary: 학교에 가요 means goes to school.
teaching_points: -에 marks the destination with 가다.
아침에 tells when the action happens.
common_mistakes: Do not choose home; 집 is home, not 학교.

questions.prompt: What should the person do?

explanation.summary: 문을 닫아 주세요 is a polite request to close the door.
teaching_points: 닫다 means close.
-아/어 주세요 asks someone to do something.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse 닫다 with 열다, which means open.

questions.prompt: Choose the best answer to the question.

explanation.summary: 이름이 뭐예요 asks what is your name, and 저는 지민이에요 gives a name.
teaching_points: 뭐예요 asks what something is.
저는 introduces the speaker politely.
common_mistakes: Do not answer with time or place; the question asks for a name.

questions.prompt: What does the notice mean?

explanation.summary: 조용히 하세요 means please be quiet.
teaching_points: 조용히 means quietly.
-세요 is a polite command or request ending.
common_mistakes: Do not read 하세요 as only do; with 조용히 the phrase means be quiet.

questions.prompt: What is the person doing now?

explanation.summary: 음악을 들어요 means listens to music.
teaching_points: 지금 means now.
듣다 means listen.

grammar.explanation: The object marker shows what is being listened to.

common_mistakes: 듣다 is listen; 쓰다 is write.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with the same meaning.

options.text: I want to buy exercise clothes.

explanation.summary: 매일 means every day, and 운동해요 means exercise.
teaching_points: 매일 is a frequency word.
하다 verbs often combine a noun plus 해요.

grammar.explanation: Many action nouns combine with 하다 to form verbs.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 매일 with 일요일; 매일 is every day.

questions.prompt: What is true about the room?

explanation.summary: 작지만 깨끗해요 means it is small but clean.
teaching_points: -지만 means but.
깨끗해요 means clean.

grammar.explanation: Connects two contrasting ideas, meaning but.

common_mistakes: Do not miss the contrast marker -지만; both small and clean are true.

questions.prompt: What is the correct order of events?

options.text: Eat breakfast, then go to school.
Go to school, then eat breakfast.

explanation.summary: 먹고 connects eating breakfast before going to school.
teaching_points: -고 can connect actions in order.
아침 can mean morning or breakfast; here 아침을 먹다 means eat breakfast.

grammar.explanation: Connects actions; often the first action happens before the second.

common_mistakes: Do not reverse the order; the verb before -고 appears first.

questions.prompt: What is the person asking?

explanation.summary: 얼마예요 asks the price, and 신발 means shoes.
teaching_points: 이 before a noun means this/these.
얼마 is used to ask how much.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse 얼마 with 어디, which asks where.

questions.prompt: What does the message ask Minji to do?
questions.passage: 민지 씨, 오늘 저녁에 전화해 주세요.

explanation.summary: 전화해 주세요 is a polite request to call, and 오늘 저녁에 means this evening.
teaching_points: 전화하다 means to call by phone.
오늘 저녁에 is a time phrase.
common_mistakes: 저녁 can mean dinner or evening; 전화해 주세요 shows the requested action is calling.

questions.prompt: When is the birthday party?
questions.passage: 생일 파티는 금요일 오후 여섯 시입니다.

explanation.summary: 금요일 오후 여섯 시 means Friday at six in the afternoon/evening.
teaching_points: 오후 means p.m.
여섯 시 uses a native Korean number for the hour.
common_mistakes: Do not miss 오후; it changes 6 to 6 p.m., not 6 a.m.

questions.prompt: What is true about Sujin?

explanation.summary: 노래를 잘해요 means sings well.
teaching_points: 잘 before a verb means well.
노래하다 means to sing; 노래를 해요 is also natural.
common_mistakes: 잘해요 is positive ability; 못해요 would mean cannot do well.

questions.prompt: What does the sign mean?

explanation.summary: 찍지 마세요 means do not take, so the sign prohibits taking photos here.
teaching_points: -지 마세요 is a polite negative command.
사진을 찍다 means to take a photo.

grammar.explanation: Polite form meaning please do not V.

common_mistakes: Do not overlook 마세요; it changes the sentence into a prohibition.

questions.prompt: What does the person want to do?

explanation.summary: 여행을 가고 싶어요 means I want to go on a trip.
teaching_points: -고 싶어요 expresses desire.
주말에 means on the weekend.
common_mistakes: Do not translate 여행 as study; it means travel.

questions.prompt: What is the person asking for?

explanation.summary: 따뜻한 차 한 잔 means one cup of warm tea.
teaching_points: 한 is one before a counter.
잔 is a counter for cups or glasses.

grammar.explanation: A common order for describing and counting nouns.

common_mistakes: Do not choose cold water; 따뜻한 means warm and 차 means tea.

questions.prompt: What does the person usually use to go to work?

explanation.summary: 버스로 가요 means goes by bus.
teaching_points: -으로 can mark the means or method of travel.
회사 means company or workplace.

grammar.explanation: Means go by N when N is a transportation method.

common_mistakes: Do not choose subway; subway is 지하철.

questions.prompt: What is the relationship between the two people?

explanation.summary: 제 동생 means my younger sibling.
teaching_points: 제 is the polite form of my.
동생 can mean younger brother or younger sister.
common_mistakes: Do not assume gender; 동생 only tells that the sibling is younger.

questions.prompt: What does the diary sentence say?

options.text: The weather was good, so I took a walk.
The weather was bad, so I stayed home.
I met a friend at the hospital.

explanation.summary: 날씨가 좋아서 gives the reason, and 산책했어요 means took a walk.
teaching_points: -아서/어서 can connect a reason to a result.
산책하다 means to take a walk.
common_mistakes: 산책 is a walk, not a mountain; 산 alone can mean mountain.

questions.prompt: What is being described?
questions.passage: 이것은 작고 빨간 과일입니다. 맛이 달아요.

options.text: A small red fruit that tastes sweet

explanation.summary: 작고 빨간 과일 means small red fruit, and 맛이 달아요 means the taste is sweet.
teaching_points: -고 connects adjectives.
달아요 describes a sweet taste.
common_mistakes: Do not choose a drink; 과일 means fruit.

questions.prompt: What is true about the store?
questions.passage: 우리 가게는 오전 열 시에 열고 오후 아홉 시에 닫습니다.

options.text: It opens at 10 a.m. and closes at 9 p.m.
It opens at 9 a.m. and closes at 10 p.m.

explanation.summary: 오전 열 시 is 10 a.m.; 오후 아홉 시 is 9 p.m.; 열다 means open and 닫다 means close.
teaching_points: 오전 means a.m. and 오후 means p.m.
열고 connects opens before the closing time.
common_mistakes: Do not swap 오전 and 오후; 오전 is before noon.

questions.prompt: What should the reader bring?
questions.passage: 내일 시험이 있습니다. 연필과 지우개를 가져오세요.

explanation.summary: 연필과 지우개를 가져오세요 means bring a pencil and an eraser.
teaching_points: 과 connects nouns in writing and formal style.
가져오세요 means please bring.
common_mistakes: Do not choose phone and wallet; the notice names school supplies.

questions.prompt: What did Yuna do last night?
questions.passage: 유나 씨는 어젯밤에 친구에게 이메일을 보냈어요.

options.text: She sent an email to a friend.
She received a gift from a teacher.
She read a book at the library.

explanation.summary: 친구에게 이메일을 보냈어요 means she sent an email to a friend.
teaching_points: -에게 can mark the recipient.
보냈어요 is the past tense of 보내다, to send.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse 보내다 with 받다; 받다 means receive.

questions.prompt: Which sentence best completes the dialogue?
questions.passage: 가: 점심을 먹었어요? 나: 아니요, 아직 안 먹었어요.

options.text: No, I have not eaten yet.
Yes, I will go to school.

explanation.summary: 아직 안 먹었어요 means I have not eaten yet.
teaching_points: 아직 means yet or still.
안 plus past tense verb gives a negative past action.
common_mistakes: 아니요 sets up a negative answer; do not choose an option beginning with yes.

questions.prompt: What does the person plan to do tomorrow?

options.text: Study together if there is time
Go shopping because there is no time

explanation.summary: 시간이 있으면 means if there is time, and 같이 공부합시다 suggests studying together.
teaching_points: -(으)면 introduces a condition.
-ㅂ시다 suggests doing something together.
common_mistakes: 있으면 means if there is; 없으면 would mean if there is not.

questions.prompt: What is the writer's problem?

options.text: The bus came late, so the writer was late to school.
The writer arrived early by bus.

explanation.summary: 늦게 와서 gives the reason that the bus came late, and 학교에 늦었어요 means was late to school.
teaching_points: 늦게 is the adverb late.
-아서/어서 connects the reason and result.
common_mistakes: Do not treat 늦게 와서 as early; 늦다 means late.

questions.prompt: What is true about the writer's family?
questions.passage: 우리 가족은 네 명입니다. 부모님, 형, 그리고 저입니다.

options.text: There are four people: parents, older brother, and the writer.
There are three people: mother, sister, and the writer.
The writer has two younger siblings.

explanation.summary: 네 명 means four people, and the list is parents, older brother, and the writer.
teaching_points: 명 is a counter for people.
형 is an older brother for a male speaker.

grammar.explanation: A formal way to state a counted amount.

common_mistakes: Do not count 부모님 as one person; it refers to both parents.

questions.prompt: What does the text say about the cafe?
questions.passage: 이 카페는 의자가 많고 커피가 맛있어요.

options.text: It has many chairs and tasty coffee.
It has no chairs and cheap tea.

explanation.summary: 의자가 많고 커피가 맛있어요 says there are many chairs and the coffee is tasty.
teaching_points: 많다 means many.
맛있어요 means delicious.
common_mistakes: 많고 means many, not none; 없다 would show none.

questions.prompt: What did the writer buy?
questions.passage: 어제 시장에서 귤 다섯 개를 샀어요.

explanation.summary: 귤 다섯 개 means five tangerines, and 샀어요 means bought.
teaching_points: 개 is a general counter for things.
다섯 is the native Korean number five.

grammar.explanation: A common counting pattern after a noun.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 귤 with 사과; 사과 is apple.

questions.prompt: What is true about the writer?
questions.passage: 저는 한국어를 배우지만 아직 잘 못해요.

options.text: The writer studies Korean but is not good at it yet.
The writer teaches Korean very well.
The writer does not want to learn Korean.

explanation.summary: 배우지만 means learns but, and 아직 잘 못해요 means cannot do it well yet.
teaching_points: -지만 marks contrast.
잘 못해요 means does not do well.
common_mistakes: 잘 못해요 is different from 잘해요; 못 makes it negative.

questions.prompt: What is the best title for the passage?
questions.passage: 저는 아침마다 공원에 가요. 공원에서 걷고 물을 마셔요. 그래서 기분이 좋아요.

explanation.summary: The passage is about going to the park every morning, walking, and feeling good.
teaching_points: 아침마다 means every morning.
The repeated topic is the morning park routine.
common_mistakes: Do not choose a title from one unrelated word; use the whole passage's main idea.

questions.prompt: What does the writer say about the bag?

options.text: It is pretty but a little heavy.

explanation.summary: 예쁘지만 조금 무거워요 means pretty but a little heavy.
teaching_points: 조금 means a little.
무거워요 means heavy.
common_mistakes: 무거워요 means heavy; 가벼워요 means light.

questions.prompt: What does the text say about the restaurant?
questions.passage: 식당은 이 층에 있습니다. 엘리베이터 옆입니다.

options.text: It is on the second floor next to the elevator.
It is on the first floor next to the restroom.

explanation.summary: 이 층 means second floor, and 엘리베이터 옆 means next to the elevator.
teaching_points: 층 is a floor counter.
옆 means beside or next to.

grammar.explanation: Means it is next to N.

common_mistakes: 이 층 as a floor is second floor; 일 층 is first floor.

questions.prompt: Why does the writer like winter?
questions.passage: 저는 겨울을 좋아해요. 눈이 오면 스키를 탈 수 있어요.

options.text: Because the writer can ski when it snows.
Because the writer can swim in the sea.

explanation.summary: 눈이 오면 스키를 탈 수 있어요 means when/if it snows, the writer can ski.
teaching_points: 눈 means snow in this context.
-(으)면 can mean if or when.
-ㄹ 수 있어요 means can do.
common_mistakes: 눈 can mean eyes or snow; 오면 shows it is snow here.

questions.prompt: What is the person going to do first?
questions.passage: 저는 먼저 숙제를 하고 친구를 만날 거예요.

explanation.summary: 먼저 숙제를 하고 means first do homework, then the writer will meet a friend.
teaching_points: 먼저 marks the first action.
-ㄹ 거예요 expresses a future plan.
common_mistakes: Do not choose the last action; 먼저 identifies the first one.

questions.prompt: What does the invitation say?
questions.passage: 토요일에 우리 집에 오세요. 같이 저녁을 먹어요.

options.text: Come to my house on Saturday and eat dinner together.
Go to school on Monday and study together.

explanation.summary: The invitation asks someone to come to the writer's house on Saturday and eat dinner together.
teaching_points: 오세요 is a polite invitation or request to come.
같이 means together.
common_mistakes: 우리 집 can mean my house in natural Korean, not only our shared house.

questions.prompt: What is true about the writer's friend?
questions.passage: 제 친구는 키가 크고 눈이 커요.

options.text: The friend is tall and has big eyes.
The friend is short and quiet.
The friend is busy and tired.
The friend is young and kind.

explanation.summary: 키가 크고 눈이 커요 describes someone as tall with big eyes.
teaching_points: 키가 크다 means to be tall.
눈이 크다 means to have big eyes.

grammar.explanation: Means N is big; with 키 it means tall.

common_mistakes: 크다 can mean tall for height and big for size; use the noun to decide.

questions.prompt: What is the best title for the passage?
questions.passage: 저는 토요일마다 한국어 학원에 가요. 두 시간 동안 한국어를 공부해요. 선생님이 친절해서 수업이 재미있어요.

explanation.summary: The passage describes going to a Korean language academy and studying Korean, so the best title is My Korean Class.
teaching_points: 학원 is a private academy.
동안 means for a length of time.

grammar.explanation: Means for a duration of time.

common_mistakes: Do not choose a title based only on 토요일; the repeated subject is Korean class.

questions.prompt: What can you infer from the note?
questions.passage: 엄마, 저는 도서관에 있어요. 여섯 시에 집에 갈게요.

options.text: The writer is at the library and will go home at six.
The writer is at home and will go to the library at six.
The writer's mother is coming to school.
The writer lost a library book.

explanation.summary: 도서관에 있어요 says the writer is at the library, and 여섯 시에 집에 갈게요 says they will go home at six.
teaching_points: -에 있어요 states location.
-ㄹ게요 can state the speaker's future intention.

grammar.explanation: States the speaker's intention or promise.

common_mistakes: Do not reverse the places; 현재 location is 도서관, future destination is 집.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the passage?
questions.passage: 저는 작은 빵집에서 일합니다. 아침에는 빵을 만들고 오후에는 손님을 만납니다. 일이 조금 힘들지만 재미있습니다.

options.text: The writer works at a small bakery and likes the work even though it is a little hard.
The writer studies at a big school and dislikes teachers.
The writer buys bread every evening because it is cheap.
The writer wants to quit because customers are not kind.

explanation.summary: The passage describes working in a small bakery, making bread, meeting customers, and finding the work hard but interesting.
teaching_points: 빵집 means bakery.
힘들지만 재미있습니다 shows contrast: hard but interesting.
common_mistakes: Do not choose an answer that mentions quitting; the passage says the work is interesting.
